name: German Integration Course Application
description: Integration course application process per BAMF requirements. Includes eligibility verification, document collection, course assignment, and completion tracking.

regulations.title: Integrationskurs - Berechtigung
regulations.summary: Defines entitlement to participate in integration courses. Foreigners with permanent residence permits or certain residence titles are entitled to participate. The course consists of a language course (600 hours) and an orientation course (100 hours).

regulations.title: Integrationskurs - Verpflichtung
regulations.summary: Establishes mandatory participation requirements. Foreigners who cannot communicate in German at a basic level may be obligated to participate. Non-compliance can affect residence permit renewal and social benefits.

regulations.title: Integrationskurs - Teilnahmebedingungen
regulations.summary: Specifies participation conditions including regular attendance requirements, examination obligations, and consequences of course discontinuation. Participants must attend at least 50% of course hours.

regulations.title: Berufsbezogene Deutschsprachförderung
regulations.summary: Covers job-related German language promotion courses that build upon integration course completion. Available for those who have completed or are completing an integration course.

regulations.title: Integrationskursverordnung - Zulassung
regulations.summary: Details the admission process for integration courses including application procedures, required documents, and processing timelines. Applications should be processed within 4 weeks.

regulations.title: Integrationskursverordnung - Kursträger
regulations.summary: Specifies requirements for course providers including accreditation standards, instructor qualifications, and quality assurance measures. Only BAMF-certified providers may conduct courses.

regulations.title: Integrationskursverordnung - Prüfungen
regulations.summary: Establishes examination requirements including the 'Deutsch-Test für Zuwanderer' (DTZ) for language proficiency and 'Leben in Deutschland' (LiD) test for orientation course completion.

regulations.title: Integrationskursverordnung - Alphabetisierungskurs
regulations.summary: Defines specialized literacy integration courses for participants who cannot read or write in their native language or Latin script. These courses have extended hours (up to 1000 hours) to accommodate literacy acquisition alongside language learning.

regulations.title: Integrationskursverordnung - Kostenbeitragsbefreiung
regulations.summary: Regulates cost exemption for integration course participants. Those receiving unemployment benefits (ALG II), social assistance, or asylum seeker benefits are exempt from course fees. Fee is 2.29 EUR per teaching hour for self-paying participants.

regulations.title: Integrationskursverordnung - Spezialkurse
regulations.summary: Establishes specialized integration courses including youth courses, parent/women courses, and intensive courses. Youth courses target participants under 27, while parent/women courses address specific needs including childcare considerations.

regulations.title: Verpflichtung zur Teilnahme am Integrationskurs
regulations.summary: Social Code provisions regarding integration course obligations for recipients of unemployment benefits. Failure to attend without good cause can result in benefit reductions (sanctions). Good causes include illness, childcare responsibilities, or care of relatives.

requiredDocuments.name: Geburtsurkunde
requiredDocuments.description: Official birth certificate from country of origin showing full name, date and place of birth, and parents' names
requiredDocuments.notes: Must be an official certified copy. If in foreign language, certified German translation by sworn translator (beeidigter Übersetzer) required. Copy should be issued within last 6 months.

requiredDocuments.name: Reisepass / Pass
requiredDocuments.description: Valid national passport showing photo, personal data, and validity dates
requiredDocuments.notes: Must be valid for at least 6 months beyond course start date. All pages must be legible. Photo page must be clearly visible.

requiredDocuments.name: Aufenthaltstitel
requiredDocuments.description: Current valid German residence permit showing permit type, validity period, and permitted activities
requiredDocuments.notes: Must be valid for duration of integration course. Eligible permit types include: recognized refugee status, humanitarian protection, family reunification, permanent residence. Address must match registration certificate.

requiredDocuments.name: Meldebescheinigung
requiredDocuments.description: Official registration certificate (Meldebescheinigung) from local registration office showing current address
requiredDocuments.notes: Must be issued within last 3 months. Must bear official stamp (Dienstsiegel) of registration office (Bürgeramt/Einwohnermeldeamt). Address must match residence permit.

requiredDocuments.name: Sprachzertifikat
requiredDocuments.description: Language certificate from recognized institution showing current German proficiency level (CEFR)
requiredDocuments.notes: Accepted institutions: Goethe-Institut, telc, TestDaF, ÖSD. Certificate should specify CEFR level (A1-C2). Used for course placement. Optional but helps determine appropriate course level.

requiredDocuments.name: Arbeitsbescheinigung
requiredDocuments.description: Employment certificate from current employer showing employment status, working hours, and position
requiredDocuments.notes: Required if employed and seeking part-time or evening course. Must show working hours to determine suitable course schedule.

requiredDocuments.name: Leistungsbescheid (ALG II / Sozialhilfe)
requiredDocuments.description: Current benefits notice from Jobcenter or Social Services showing benefit type and amount
requiredDocuments.notes: Required for cost exemption (Kostenbeitragsbefreiung). Accepted benefits: ALG II (Arbeitslosengeld II), Sozialhilfe, Leistungen nach AsylbLG. Must be current (dated within last 3 months).

requiredDocuments.name: Krankenversicherungskarte
requiredDocuments.description: Valid German health insurance card (gesetzlich or privat)
requiredDocuments.notes: Both statutory (gesetzlich) and private (privat) health insurance accepted. Card must show current validity. Required to enroll in course.

requiredDocuments.name: Biometrisches Passfoto
requiredDocuments.description: Recent biometric passport photo meeting official standards
requiredDocuments.notes: Must be taken within last 6 months. Specifications: 35x45mm, neutral facial expression, white/light gray background, no head covering (religious exceptions allowed), front-facing view.

requiredDocuments.name: Antrag auf Zulassung zum Integrationskurs
requiredDocuments.description: Completed BAMF application form for integration course admission (Form 630.007w)
requiredDocuments.notes: Official BAMF form 630.007w must be used. All sections must be completed legibly. Must include applicant signature and current date. Course preferences (location, time, type) should be indicated.

requiredDocuments.name: Teilnahmebescheinigungen früherer Kurse
requiredDocuments.description: Certificates from any previously attended German language or integration courses
requiredDocuments.notes: Include if applicant previously attended integration courses, German language courses, or orientation courses. Must show: course provider, course type, hours attended, completion status or reason for discontinuation.

requiredDocuments.name: Verpflichtung / Überweisung
requiredDocuments.description: Official referral or obligation letter from immigration office (Ausländerbehörde) or Jobcenter
requiredDocuments.notes: Required only if obligated to attend per §44 AufenthG. Must be issued by competent authority (Ausländerbehörde, Jobcenter, or BAMF). Must specify reason for obligation and deadline for enrollment.

requiredDocuments.name: Eheurkunde / Heiratsurkunde
requiredDocuments.description: Official marriage certificate if applicant is married
requiredDocuments.notes: Required if marital status relevant for case (e.g., family reunification context, spouse also attending). Must be certified copy with German translation if in foreign language.

requiredDocuments.name: Geburtsurkunden der Kinder
requiredDocuments.description: Birth certificates of dependent children if relevant for childcare considerations
requiredDocuments.notes: Required for parent/women courses with childcare component. Helps determine need for courses with childcare provision or flexible scheduling.

requiredDocuments.name: Schulzeugnisse / Bildungsnachweise
requiredDocuments.description: Educational certificates showing completed education levels (school, vocational, university)
requiredDocuments.notes: Helps determine appropriate course level. Particularly important for literacy course assessment. Shows whether applicant literate in native language. German translation required for foreign certificates.

requiredDocuments.name: Ärztliches Attest
requiredDocuments.description: Medical certificate documenting health conditions, disabilities, or need for accommodations
requiredDocuments.notes: Required if applicant needs special accommodations (wheelchair access, hearing assistance, visual aids) or has medical reasons for course modifications. Must be from licensed German doctor, dated within 3 months.

requiredDocuments.name: Nachweis Kinderbetreuung
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of childcare arrangements during course hours (Kita-Platz, Tagesmutter)
requiredDocuments.notes: Important for scheduling. Shows feasibility of course attendance. Alternatively, inquire about courses with integrated childcare (Frauenkurse/Elternkurse mit Kinderbetreuung).

validationRules.condition: Applicant must be at least 18 years old or have parental consent if minor
validationRules.action: Calculate age from birthDate, verify >= 18 or check for parental consent document

validationRules.condition: Applicant must hold residence permit type eligible for integration course (§43 AufenthG)
validationRules.action: Verify residence permit type against eligible categories: permanent residence, humanitarian protection, family reunification, recognized refugees

validationRules.condition: All identity documents must be valid and not expired
validationRules.action: Check expiry dates on passport, residence permit; flag if expiring within 3 months

validationRules.condition: Address must be consistent across all documents
validationRules.action: Compare addresses on registration certificate, residence permit, and application form

validationRules.condition: Name spelling must match across all documents
validationRules.action: Verify name on birth certificate matches passport and residence permit; note transliteration variations

validationRules.condition: If language certificate provided, verify CEFR level for integration course placement
validationRules.action: Check certificate level (A1-C2), verify issuing institution is BAMF-recognized for integration courses

validationRules.condition: Previous integration course participation must be documented if applicable
validationRules.action: Check for previous course certificates, verify completion status or discontinuation reason

validationRules.condition: If obligated to attend integration course (§44 AufenthG), verify referral documentation
validationRules.action: Check for official referral letter from Ausländerbehörde or Jobcenter with integration course obligation notice

validationRules.condition: Foreign language documents require certified German translation
validationRules.action: Verify all non-German documents have attached certified translation with translator credentials

validationRules.condition: Passport photo must meet biometric standards
validationRules.action: Verify photo dimensions, background color, face visibility, and recency (< 6 months)

commonIssues.suggestion: All non-German documents require certified translation by a sworn translator (beeidigter Übersetzer). Provide translator name, certification number, and stamp. Cost typically 10-50 EUR per page.
Residence permit must be valid. If permit is expiring soon, advise applicant to apply for extension at Ausländerbehörde before integration course begins. Processing takes 4-8 weeks.
All mandatory fields must be completed including personal details, contact information, course preferences, and signature. Use official BAMF form 630.007w available at www.bamf.de
Registration certificate address must match current residence. If recently moved, applicant needs new Meldebescheinigung from local Bürgeramt. Process takes 1-2 weeks.
Different transliterations of non-Latin names are common. Document variations but ensure primary spelling matches official residence permit. Consider adding 'auch bekannt als' (also known as) note.
Only BAMF-recognized institutions accepted for integration course placement: Goethe-Institut, telc, TestDaF, ÖSD. Other certificates may not be accepted for placement assessment.

If applicant previously discontinued an integration course, documentation of reason required. Medical reasons need doctor's certificate. Personal reasons need explanation letter. May affect funding eligibility.
Indicating preferred course type (intensive, part-time, evening) and location helps with faster placement in integration courses. Consider work schedule, childcare needs, and travel time.
If passport expires during 6-12 month course period, advise renewal at embassy/consulate before course start. Integration course certificate (DTZ/LiD) requires valid ID at examination.

To receive cost exemption (Kostenbeitragsbefreiung), must provide current benefits notice (ALG II, Sozialhilfe, AsylbLG). Standard fee is 2.29 EUR per teaching hour (total ~1600 EUR for 700-hour course).

commonIssues.issue: Photo does not meet biometric standards
commonIssues.suggestion: Biometric photo requirements: 35x45mm, taken within 6 months, neutral expression, white/light gray background, no sunglasses, facing forward. Photo booths at post offices, train stations, and photo studios provide compliant photos.
Meldebescheinigung must be current (issued within 3 months). Obtain new certificate from local Bürgeramt/Einwohnermeldeamt. Usually available same day or within 1 week.

Health insurance coverage is mandatory for course enrollment. If card expired or invalid, contact health insurance provider (gesetzliche Krankenkasse) immediately for replacement. Cannot start course without valid insurance.

commonIssues.issue: Wrong residence permit type for entitlement
commonIssues.suggestion: Not all residence permit types grant automatic entitlement to integration course. Check §43 AufenthG eligibility. If not entitled, can still apply for admission per §44 AufenthG if places available.

commonIssues.issue: Application submitted to wrong BAMF regional office
commonIssues.suggestion: Applications must be submitted to BAMF regional office responsible for applicant's place of residence. Use BAMF-NAvI tool on www.bamf.de to find correct office. Wrong office will return application causing delays.

commonIssues.issue: Missing official stamp on registration certificate
commonIssues.suggestion: Meldebescheinigung must bear official stamp (Dienstsiegel) of issuing Bürgeramt/Einwohnermeldeamt. Simple address confirmation letters are not sufficient. Request official stamped certificate.

If obligated to attend per §44 AufenthG, referral letter specifies enrollment deadline. Missing deadline can result in residence permit issues or benefit sanctions per §44a SGB II. Contact issuing authority immediately if deadline approaching.

Parents with young children should inquire about Elternkurse (parent courses) or Frauenkurse (women's courses) that include childcare. Alternatively, arrange external childcare before course start to ensure regular attendance.

If applicant not literate in Latin script or native language, requires Alphabetisierungskurs (literacy course) per IntV §6. These courses have 1000 hours instead of standard 700. Provide education certificates to help assessment.

Applicants with disabilities, chronic illnesses, or special needs should provide medical certificate detailing required accommodations (wheelchair access, hearing loop, visual aids, extra time). Course providers can make arrangements if informed in advance.

commonIssues.issue: Employment schedule conflicts with course times
commonIssues.suggestion: Employed applicants should provide work schedule to find compatible course (part-time, evening, weekend). Some course providers offer flexible schedules. Discuss with employer possibility of paid educational leave (Bildungsurlaub).

If experiencing issues with online application portal, use paper form 630.007w and submit by mail to BAMF regional office. Include all required documents as certified copies. Keep proof of mailing (Einschreiben recommended).

Certified translations must include: translator's full name, certification number, official stamp, signature, and date. Translation by friend/family member not accepted. Find sworn translators at local court (Landgericht) or via Bundesverband der Dolmetscher und Übersetzer (BDÜ).

High demand may result in waiting lists. Apply as early as possible. If obligated per §44 AufenthG, obligation is fulfilled by timely application. While waiting, consider voluntary German courses at Volkshochschule (VHS) or community organizations.

courseTypes.eligibility: For participants not literate in Latin script
